metadata.title: Earth Air Tunnel Geothermal Cooling Exchanger

public_links.title: Wikipedia: Ground-to-Air Heat Exchanger (GAHE) Design & Mechanics
Appropedia: Open-Source Earth-Air Tunnel Geothermal Cooling Systems
ScienceDirect: Performance Evaluation and Thermal Modeling of Earth-Air Pipe Heat Exchangers

technical_documents.description: An off-grid, low-enthalpy geothermal cooling infrastructure blueprint designed to reduce residential cooling loads. By routing fresh atmospheric intake through a deep subsurface pipe matrix, this system leverages the earth's constant thermal inertia to pre-cool high-temperature air streams without mechanical compressors or chemical refrigerants.
technical_documents.problem_statement: Conventional mechanical air conditioning accounting for over 15% of peak electrical grid demand in rapidly urbanizing developing regions. With high-end commercial ground-source geothermal HVAC installations costing upwards of $5,000, over 90% of local households are financially excluded from sustainable climate adaptation measures.
technical_documents.engineering_solution: The system configures a 20-metre high-density polyethylene (HDPE) corrugated conduit path buried at a subsurface lithospheric depth of 3 meters, where soil temperatures maintain a constant annual baseline of 14°C. Driven by a low-draw 15W solar-powered DC axial fan, ambient 35°C air is drawn through this underground matrix. Convective heat transfer across the high-surface-area corrugated boundaries drops air intake temperatures down to 18–20°C at the building manifold, delivering up to 2,000 CFM of pre-cooled fresh air to a 30 sqm thermal envelope using minimal power.

bill_of_materials.name: High-Density Polyethylene (HDPE) Corrugated Heat-Exchange Conduit (150mm x 20m)
15W DC Solar-Powered Axial Fan Assembly & 20W Photovoltaic Panel
Heavy-Duty Intake Fluid Filter Box & Fine Anti-Insect Mesh Screen
Rigid PVC Airway Elbows, Adaptors, & Sealed Couplings
Sub-grade Condensate Fluid Drain Trap & Gravity Evacuation Assembly

specifications.thermal_exchange: Ground-coupled geothermal cooling via 3m deep subsurface mass matrix

step_by_step_build: Step 1: Excavate a 3m deep trench, 20m long, sloping at 2% away from the building for condensate drainage.
Step 2: Lay HDPE pipe with 150mm gravel bed underneath; backfill with excavated soil, compacting in 30cm lifts.
Step 3: Install inlet filter box at the far end with fine insect mesh to prevent rodent entry.
Step 4: Mount the 15W solar fan at the building wall penetration; wire to a 20W panel with a small charge controller.
Step 5: Add a condensate drain trap at the lowest point of the pipe run; test airflow with a tissue at room vent.

comments.text: Incredible ROI. Our 40°C Rajasthan summer air comes out at 22°C. Built for under 80 XLM total.
How do you handle condensation and mould inside the pipe over long periods?
